item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon as Iran says it "cannot trust the Americans"
item.description: Two days of talks in Washington between Lebanon and Israel produced an extension of the current ceasefire by 45 days.

item.title: Atlanta residents report Waymo traffic jams
item.description: Atlanta residents say they're waking up to traffic jams of self-driving Waymo vehicles confused by neighborhood signage.

item.title: Ice cream sold in 17 states recalled for potential metal fragments
item.description: No injuries have been reported because of the issue, the Food and Drug Administration said.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Taj Mahal performs "Time"
item.description: Five-time Grammy winner and blues legend Taj Mahal is best known for hits like "Corinna" and "Statesboro Blues," and has collaborated with The Rolling Stones and Eric Clapton during his career that spans six decades. His latest album, "Time," was recorded with his long-time bandmates "The Phantom Blues Band." Here's Taj Mahal performing "Time."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Taj Mahal performs "Queen Bee"
item.description: Five-time Grammy winner and blues legend Taj Mahal is best known for hits like "Corinna" and "Statesboro Blues," and has collaborated with The Rolling Stones and Eric Clapton during his career that spans six decades. His latest album, "Time," was recorded with his long-time bandmates "The Phantom Blues Band." Here's Taj Mahal performing "Queen Bee."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Taj Mahal performs "Wild About My Lovin'"
item.description: Five-time Grammy winner and blues legend Taj Mahal is best known for hits like "Corinna" and "Statesboro Blues," and has collaborated with The Rolling Stones and Eric Clapton during his career that spans six decades. His latest album, "Time," was recorded with his long-time bandmates "The Phantom Blues Band." Here's Taj Mahal performing "Wild About My Lovin'."

item.title: Long Island Rail Road workers go on strike after failing to reach new contract
item.description: The work stoppage, the LIRR's first since 1994, went into effect after the MTA and five unions representing 3,500 workers failed to reach a deal on a new four-year contract.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Virginia Democrats' bid to revive new congressional map
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday rejected a bid by Virginia Democrats to revive its new voter-approved congressional map that was drawn to advantage the party for the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: Devastating wildfires rip through Texas panhandle
item.description: Wildfires fueled by dry conditions and high winds are ripping through Texas and other southwestern states as firefighters struggle to contain the blaze.

item.title: Days after Trump's summit in Beijing, Putin will meet with China's Xi
item.description: Russia's Putin will travel to Beijing next week to meet China's Xi. The announcement came one day after President Trump returned from his own summit with the Chinese leader.

item.title: Uncertainty remains over Taiwan after Trump returns from China
item.description: President Trump returned from his high-stakes China trip, but uncertainty remains over a potential $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan.

item.title: Concern grows in central Africa over deadly Ebola virus
item.description: A deadly strain of Ebola virus has sparked concern in the Democratic Republic of Congo amid clashes between rival militia groups.

item.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday.

item.title: Court records on arrested Iraqi national reveal details of foiled terror plots targetting Jews
item.description: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, an Iraqi national, is facing federal charges for his alleged terror plots targeting Jewish institutions around the U.S.

item.title: The Uplift: Derby dream come true
item.description: David Begnaud meets a man who has attended the Kentucky Derby for 79 years in a row – and his dying wish to make it there one last time.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 17): "By Design"
item.description: "Sunday Morning" presents its annual edition touching on all aspects of design, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: GOP Sen. Cassidy fights to hold onto seat in Louisiana primary
item.description: Louisianans are voting Saturday in the state's Senate primaries as Sen. Bill Cassidy fights to hold onto his seat, facing a Trump-backed primary challenger.

item.title: Rescue diver dies amid search for remains of 4 who perished in Maldives accident
item.description: Five people died in the scuba diving accident. Divers are still searching for the remains of four victims in an underwater cave.

item.title: Beverly Hills 911 | Post Mortem
item.description: CBS News correspondent Tracy Smith and "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty discuss the case of 67-year-old Violet Yacobi, who was found dead by her children in her Beverly Hills mansion.

item.title: Hamas leader who helped plan Oct. 7 attacks has been killed, Israel says
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed in a "precise strike in the area on the City of Gaza," the Israel Defense Forces said Saturday.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Preakness Stakes
item.description: The Preakness Stakes will feature its biggest field in 15 years today with 14 horses in the middle jewel of horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: A hospital worker felt healthy. Then he had 3 heart attacks in 4 days.
item.description: Tommy Bell thought he was in good shape. But a series of heart attacks painted a more complicated picture.

item.title: Controversial FDA official leaving drug center post in latest departure at agency
item.description: Dr. Tracy Beth Høeg, leader of the Food and Drug Administration division responsible for regulating prescription and over-the-counter drugs, is leaving her post, a senior FDA official confirmed.

item.title: Trump says U.S. has killed Islamic State leader in Nigeria
item.description: President Trump announced Friday evening that U.S. and Nigerian military forces had killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, a leader in the Islamic State group.

item.title: New York doctor who survived Ebola says he fears for healthcare workers
item.description: A New York doctor who contracted and survived Ebola more than a decade ago says he is worried for healthcare workers who are at the center of treating the latest outbreak.

item.title: Colorado governor commutes Trump ally Tina Peters' prison sentence
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis announced he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, who was facing more than eight years in state prison for allowing unauthorized access to voting machines following the 2020 presidential election.

item.title: A longtime Atlanta principal retired, then returned to his school as a handyman
item.description: David White retired as the longtime principal at the Burgess-Peterson Academy in Atlanta, and then returned to the school as its handyman.

item.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
item.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

item.title: SpaceX launches cargo ship to International Space Station
item.description: The International Space Station-bound SpaceX Cargo Dragon is loaded with 6,500 pounds of needed equipment, research gear and crew supplies.

item.title: Pentagon's rush to counter drone threat may pose safety risks, specialist says
item.description: The safety specialist's warning appeared in a memo describing how a mini-drone had detonated and injured an Army Special Forces soldier.
